headline: Prime Video Fetches Hot Aussie Canine Reality Format 'Muster Dogs'
lede: Wonder Project has secured the U.S. remake rights to Australia's breakout canine reality series, signaling growing international appetite for unscripted animal programming.
body:
Prime Video is bringing Down Under dog drama stateside with an American adaptation of ABC's surprise hit *Muster Dogs*. The unscripted series, which tracks working dogs and their handlers in the rugged Australian countryside, represents a fresh take on reality competition formats.

Wonder Project's acquisition highlights the growing global market for animal-centric unscripted content. The original Australian series garnered significant audience engagement by showcasing the extraordinary skills of working dogs in agricultural settings, blending competition dynamics with authentic rural storytelling.

While specific casting and production details remain under wraps, industry observers anticipate the U.S. version will maintain the original's core narrative of skill, training, and canine-human partnership. The format's success stems from its unique blend of performance evaluation and emotional storytelling, tracking dogs' remarkable capabilities in challenging environments.

The international format trade continues to demonstrate robust cross-border entertainment potential, with unscripted concepts traveling more frequently than scripted properties. *Muster Dogs* joins a growing roster of reality formats successfully reimagined for different international markets.
key_facts: Wonder Project acquired U.S. remake rights
Original series aired on Australian Broadcasting Corporation
Format focuses on working dogs in agricultural settings
Prime Video to develop U.S. adaptation
